pages.raw_text:
A vigorous protest will be made
with the city council shortly by the
First Ward Improvement club
against floods in the Union Park
district.


Councilman Schaefer will file a
resolution with the city council to-
night, demanding grade separation
from the n. P. through Spokane.


One hundred voters gathered at
the city hall last night and decided
to hold a mass meeting tomorrow
night at the Spokane theater, pro-
testing against the site selected for
the new city hall.


A motorman on the Mantle street
car line tainted while at his post
yesterday and just barely managed
to throw on the air clutch before
losing consciousness, the car travel
ing down the Washington street
hill at a high rate of speed, but
stopping before the switch was
reached.


When Or. l.. p. Waterh ap-
pears in the police court this after
noon, he will have to explain why
he failed to report case of scarlet
fever.


Special Officer Hope got the drop
on w. A. Newman, a hobo. who was
stealing a ride on the \. P. fast
mail, and brought him to the police
station.


At meeting of the Liberty Park
Improvement club last night Con
tractor Fl. l.. Lilienthal made things
lively for a while by vigorously pro-
testing against the action of the
club in calling on the board of
works a couple of weeks ago and
scoring his alleged dilatory meth
DDS of street construction,


Gordon r. vauthrin, aged 81
years, died of scarlet fever yester-
day morning at the Sacred Heart
hospital. The body is at the Blu
chanan undertaking rooms awaiting
the arrival of the father.


Charles w. Framer, aged 30
years, died yesterday morning at
the home OF his son-in-law, John A.
Wilson, S1S23 Maple street. He
came here three weeks ago from
Montezuma, Iowa. The body will
be sent by the Turnbull Undertak-
ing company this morning to his for
mer home.


Beginning Sunday, July 81, the
Northern Pacific railway train No.
2, eastbound, will carry passengers
from Seattle, Tacoma, Portland, FL
iensburg. North Yakima and this
city for Miles City, Mont.


George A. Bond was yesterday APP
pointed assistant secretary of the
national apple show to assist Secre-
tary Rem ii. Rice in preparations
OF final details. Mr.. Bond is the
owner Of an orchard and poultry
farm at Meadow Lake and occupied
the same position at the first apple
ssllow In 1908. He began his duties
yesterday.


The body Of Mrs. Anna L. Wilson,
